taxonomy: Ctenitopsis sinii is a species within the genus Ctenitopsis, which is a part of the family Dryopteridaceae. This family consists of ferns, specifically the wood ferns and lady ferns. Ctenitopsis sinii falls under the class Polypodiopsida, which is a group of vascular plants that includes ferns. The class is further divided into the division Pteridophyta, which represents all vascular plants that produce spores. intro: Ctenitopsis sinii, also known as Chinese Broomrape, is a type of parasitic plant that derives its nutrients by attaching itself to the roots of other plants, typically legumes. The plant has been traditionally used in Chinese medicine for its purported health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitopsis sinii include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In traditional Chinese herbal medicine, Ctenitopsis sinii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets.
